𝗠𝗢𝗕𝗜𝗟𝗘-𝗠𝗖𝗣 | To provide a unified, platform-agnostic interface for automating native iOS and Android applications on various device types, enabling LLM-driven interactions and complex user journeys.  🔥 Super Hot!

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a unified, platform-agnostic interface for automating native iOS and Android applications on various device types, enabling LLM-driven interactions and complex user journeys.
📅 Created: Mar 2025 • Updated: Mar 2026

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Platform-agnostic interface for iOS and Android automation
✅ Supports emulators, simulators, and real devices
✅ Interacts with native applications via accessibility snapshots or coordinate-based taps
✅ Enables LLM-driven automation and agent-to-agent communication for mobile tasks
✅ Fast and lightweight with fallback to screenshot analysis when accessibility data is unavailable


✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 340 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 26 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

The Mobile MCP server acts as a platform-agnostic interface for automating and interacting with native iOS and Android applications. It allows agents and LLMs to control mobile devices (emulators, simulators, and real devices) using accessibility data or screenshot-based analysis, simplifying mobile development and automation tasks.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/mobile-next/mobile-mcp

𝗖𝗘𝗥𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗔𝗧𝗘 | Automating and simplifying the management of SSL certificates for self-hosted environments through a visual interface.  🔥 Super Hot!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐹 Go 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automating and simplifying the management of SSL certificates for self-hosted environments through a visual interface.
📅 Created: Aug 2024 • Updated: Mar 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Automated SSL certificate issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring
✅ Self-hosted with full data control and privacy
✅ Zero dependencies, single-click deployment
✅ Low resource usage (~16MB memory)
✅ User-friendly GUI for visual workflow orchestration
✅ Supports single/multiple/wildcard domain and IP certificates
✅ Supports RSA or ECC key types
✅ Supports DNS-01 and HTTP-01 challenges
✅ Supports PEM, PFX, JKS certificate formats
✅ Integrates with 60+ domain registrars
✅ Integrates with 110+ deployment targets
✅ Multiple notification channels (email, Slack, Discord, etc.)
✅ Supports multiple ACME CAs (Let's Encrypt, ZeroSSL, etc.)


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 8.4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 798 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 50 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Certimate is an open-source, self-hosted ACME tool that automates the entire lifecycle of SSL certificate management, including issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring. It offers a user-friendly GUI, zero dependencies, and low resource usage, making it suitable for various deployment scenarios.

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/certimate-go/certimate

𝗢𝗣𝗘𝗡𝗠𝗘𝗠𝗢𝗥𝗬 | To provide AI agents and LLM applications with persistent, long-term memory, enabling them to retain information and context across interactions without relying solely on stateless models or basic RAG.  🌟 Rising Fast!

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide AI agents and LLM applications with persistent, long-term memory, enabling them to retain information and context across interactions without relying solely on stateless models or basic RAG.
📅 Created: Oct 2025 • Updated: Mar 2026

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Real long-term memory for AI agents
✅ Self-hosted, local-first storage (SQLite/Postgres)
✅ Python and Node.js SDKs
✅ Integrations with LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Streamlit, MCP, VS Code
✅ Connectors for GitHub, Notion, Google Drive, OneDrive, Web Crawler
✅ Explainable traces for memory recall


♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 3.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 419 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 10 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

OpenMemory is a self-hosted, local-first cognitive memory engine for AI agents, providing real long-term memory beyond simple embeddings. It offers SDKs for Python and Node.js, integrates with popular agent frameworks, and supports data ingestion from various sources.

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦

𝗦𝗚𝗟𝗔𝗡𝗚 | Serving large language models and multimodal models with high performance and low latency for inference.  📈 Growing!

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Serving large language models and multimodal models with high performance and low latency for inference.
📅 Created: Jan 2024 • Updated: Mar 2026

✅ High-performance inference for LLMs and multimodal models
✅ Optimized attention mechanisms (e.g., FlashAttention, PagedAttention)
✅ Support for a wide array of open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, etc.)
✅ Day-0 support for new model releases
✅ Multi-GPU and distributed inference capabilities
✅ Support for various backends including CUDA and Jax (TPU)

SGLang is a high-performance serving framework designed for efficient inference of large language models (LLMs) and multimodal models. It offers advanced features like optimized attention mechanisms and supports a wide range of popular models, enabling faster and more scalable AI deployments.

𝗫𝟰𝟬𝟮 | Enabling seamless, network-agnostic payments over the internet using HTTP.  ✨ Notable!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Enabling seamless, network-agnostic payments over the internet using HTTP.
📅 Created: Feb 2025 • Updated: Mar 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Open standard for internet-native payments
✅ Supports all networks (crypto & fiat) and forms of value (stablecoins, tokens, fiat)
✅ Built on HTTP, complementing existing data transportation
✅ Network, token, and currency agnostic
✅ Backwards compatible
✅ Trust minimizing
✅ Easy to use, abstracting crypto complexities


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 1.3K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 200 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

x402 is an open standard for internet-native payments, built on HTTP. It aims to support all networks and forms of value, abstracting away complexities like gas fees for clients and resource servers.
